alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing an anilide group in which the carboxamide group is substituted with an aromatic group. They have the general structure RNC(=O)R', where R= benzene, and R = aryl group.
alternative_parents.name: Aromatic anilides

alternative_parents.description: Naphthalene derivatives carrying one or more hydroxyl (-OH) groups at any ring position.
alternative_parents.name: Naphthols and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a 2-hydroxybenzoic acid moiety or a derivative thereof.
alternative_parents.name: Salicylic acid and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Benzoic acids (or derivatives) which bear a sulfanyl group (R-SH) attached to the benzene ring at positions 1 and 3, respectively.
alternative_parents.name: M-sulfanylbenzoic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Thioether derivatives of thiophenols. Thiophenol is a phenol derivative obtained by replacing the oxygen atom from the hydroxyl group (attached to the benzene) by a sulfur atom.
alternative_parents.name: Thiophenol ethers

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds contaning a phenoxy group.
alternative_parents.name: Phenoxy compounds

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing an ether group substituted with a benzene ring.
alternative_parents.name: Phenol ethers

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a benzene ring which bears a methane group.
alternative_parents.name: Toluenes

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the alkyl aryl ether functional group with the generic formula R-O-R' , where R is an alkyl group and R' is an aryl group.
alternative_parents.name: Alkyl aryl ethers

alternative_parents.description: Organosulfur compounds containing a thioether group that is substituted by an alkyl and an aryl group.
alternative_parents.name: Alkylarylthioethers

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a hydroxyl group, which is indirectly attached to a carbonyl via an intervening vinyl (>C=C<) moiety.
alternative_parents.name: Vinylogous acids

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a secondary carboxylic acid amide functional group, with the general structure RC(=O)N(R')H (R,R'=alkyl, aryl).
alternative_parents.name: Secondary carboxylic acid amides

alternative_parents.description: Organosulfur compounds a sulfenyl group with the general formula RS (R = organyl).
alternative_parents.name: Sulfenyl compounds

alternative_parents.description: Carboxylic acids containing exactly one carboxyl groups.
alternative_parents.name: Monocarboxylic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a carboxylic acid group with the formula -C(=O)OH.
alternative_parents.name: Carboxylic acids

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a carbonyl group, with the general structure RC(=O)R', where R=organyl, R'=H, N, O, organyl group or halide group.
alternative_parents.name: Carbonyl compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a nitrogen atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organonitrogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

class.description: Compounds containing a naphthalene moiety, which consists of two fused benzene rings.
class.name: Naphthalenes

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as 1-hydroxynaphthalene-2-carboxanilides. These are naphthalene-2-carboxamides, where the carboxamide group is substituted with an aniline, and the naphthalene ring system is hydroxylated at the ring 1-position.

direct_parent.description: Naphthalene-2-carboxamides, where the carboxamide group is substituted with an aniline, and the naphthalene ring system is hydroxylated at the ring 1-position.
direct_parent.name: 1-hydroxynaphthalene-2-carboxanilides

intermediate_nodes.description: Compounds containing a naphthalene moiety, which bears a carboxylic acid amide group at one or more positions. Naphthalene is a bicyclic compound that is made up of two fused benzene rings.
intermediate_nodes.name: Naphthalenecarboxamides

intermediate_nodes.description: Naphthalene-2-carboxamides, where the carboxamide group is substituted with an aniline.
intermediate_nodes.name: Naphthalene-2-carboxanilides

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Compounds containing a naphthalene moiety, which bears a carboxylic acid group or a derivative at one or more positions. Naphthalene is a bicyclic compound that is made up of two fused benzene ring.
subclass.name: Naphthalenecarboxylic acids and derivatives

superclass.description: Aromatic compounds containing one or more benzene rings.
superclass.name: Benzenoids
